response.justification: James Smith has extensive experience in C/C++ (e.g., National Instruments, NI LabWindows/CVI), but his recent roles primarily focus on full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript and other languages like D. His lack of recent C++ cloud/on-premises industrial automation experience and no explicit mention of IEC 62443 or secure development lifecycle practices weaken his fit for this role. However, his strong C++ background and problem-solving skills justify a 'maybe' interview recommendation.
strengths: Strong C/C++ experience (e.g., NI LabWindows/CVI, low-latency D applications)
Proven ability to design and deliver high-performance, scalable systems (e.g., micro-services at Sociomantic, GISCollective platform)
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with a focus on software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, refactoring)
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience in industrial automation/cloud contexts (last C++ role was 2015)
No explicit mention of IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
Lack of direct experience with message brokers, MQTT, or REST APIs in industrial automation

key_factors.factor: Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and IEC 62443 knowledge
Recent hands-on C++ development in relevant domains
